meaning: n.起抑制作用的因素,节气闸,断音装置
examples: The cold weather put a damper on our plans.
This was a damper, and conversation died again.
For the next 1,500 years, its inhabitants enjoyed a somewhat warmer and damper climate than today, living in a well-wooded steppe area where wild cereal grasses were abundant.
He installed a horizontal piston that rides along the fork as a steering damper.
Well, it certainly puts a considerable damper on their first undefeated season since 1988.
